task: Write a Python function range_summary(nums) taking a list of integers, possibly unsorted with duplicates, and returning a string of the distinct values in ascending order, collapsing consecutive runs into 'start-end' and joining pieces with commas, so [1, 2, 3, 5] becomes '1-3,5'. A single number stays a single number, never a range. An empty list returns ''. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The solution meets all the criteria and handles an empty list correctly.
trace: I first handle the edge case of an empty list by returning an empty string. Then, I sort and deduplicate the input list to ensure each number is considered only once and they are in ascending order. Using a loop, I iterate through the sorted unique numbers, identifying consecutive ranges by checking if the current number is exactly one more than the previous end. If a range ends, it's added to the summary parts in the required format ('start-end' or just 'number'). After the loop, I add the last segment and join all parts with commas.
